text: The surgeon begins the sacral dissection by making a small incision in the peritoneum overlying the sacrum, ensuring the incision is distal to the bifurcation of the common iliac arteries.

The surgeon uses a PK device to press down and locate the sacral promontory, identifying finger-like projections of presacral fat. The surgeon finds a window between these projections to approach the anterior longitudinal ligament.
